stories.headline: Trump pressures Netanyahu to halt Iran retaliation, says 'I call the shots' as strikes pause
stories.summary: After Iran and Israel traded missile fire over the weekend for the first time since the April truce, President Trump phoned Netanyahu to demand he hold off further strikes, warning Israel could be 'on your own' if war resumed. Trump told the Financial Times that Netanyahu 'won't have any choice' but to accept a US-Iran deal and that 'I call the shots.' Both sides paused attacks as Trump said an immediate ceasefire and final peace talks were proceeding.

perspectives.keyFraming: The US president casts himself as the decisive actor who brokered the de-escalation and is steering both parties toward a final deal.
perspectives.summary: Trump publicly framed himself as in charge, saying he 'made' Netanyahu stop and that 'I call the shots. I call all the shots.' He posted that both Israel and Iran were 'looking to do an immediate CEASEFIRE' with final peace negotiations proceeding, while warning the Hormuz blockade stays in place until a final deal is reached.

sources.title: Trump tells Netanyahu not to strike Iran

perspectives.keyFraming: A fragile US-brokered ceasefire nearly collapsed on the war's 100th day before both sides pulled back.
perspectives.summary: Iran launched 'Operation Nasr' missile barrages and Israel struck back in the most serious confrontation since the April truce. Iran said it was halting attacks after Trump called for the shooting to stop, but warned of harsher action if Israel kept striking Lebanon. Reporting noted disagreements inside Netanyahu's cabinet over complying with Trump's demand.

sources.title: The Latest: Israel and Iran trade fire in most serious confrontation since April truce
Israel and Iran pull back after trading missile fire — for now

perspectives.keyFraming: Peace talks are at a standstill even as Trump works to keep the ceasefire alive amid renewed fighting.
perspectives.summary: Fox's live coverage framed the exchange as a stall in negotiations, with Israel and Iran trading missiles and the Strait of Hormuz still in play. Trump demanded all sides stop shooting and insisted negotiations continue, while warning Netanyahu personally to stand down.

sources.title: Iran peace talks at standstill as Israel trades missiles with Tehran

perspectives.keyFraming: Trump's on-again-off-again ceasefire underscores the volatility of his personalized Iran diplomacy.
perspectives.summary: CNN's live reporting documented Iran and Israel halting strikes against each other while both warned they were ready to resume, casting doubt on the durability of Trump's claimed ceasefire progress.

sources.title: Live updates: Iran and Israel halt strikes against each other but warn they are ready to resume

stories.headline: Israeli strike on Lebanon's Tyre kills five as Iran warns over Lebanon; thousands flee Beirut suburbs
stories.summary: Even as Iran and Israel paused their direct strikes, Israel said it would keep hitting Hezbollah in Lebanon. An Israeli strike on Tyre on Monday killed five and wounded eight, per Lebanon's Health Ministry. Thousands fled Beirut's southern Dahieh after Israel announced renewed strikes, with the UN reporting families leaving by car, motorcycle and on foot. Iran warned it would resume attacks on Israel if strikes on Lebanon continued.

perspectives.keyFraming: Israel decouples its Lebanon operations from the Iran pause, drawing an explicit Iranian warning.
perspectives.summary: Israel said it would continue 'full intensity' operations against Hezbollah even after halting strikes on Iran; a strike on Tyre killed five. Iran tied any resumption of its own attacks to Israeli restraint in Lebanon, keeping the broader truce fragile.

sources.title: Live updates: Iran and Israel halt strikes against each other but warn they are ready to resume

perspectives.keyFraming: The humanitarian toll mounts as civilians again flee under threat of renewed strikes.
perspectives.summary: UN agencies reported thousands fleeing Beirut's southern suburbs after Israeli warnings of renewed strikes, with UNHCR describing families leaving on foot carrying essentials. More than a million Lebanese have been displaced and over 3,600 killed since the war's escalation in March.

sources.title: Lebanon: Families flee Beirut as Israel threatens renewed strikes on Hezbollah

perspectives.keyFraming: Israel says the strikes target Hezbollah commanders and militants after drone attacks on its forces.
perspectives.summary: Israel said its Lebanon operations target Hezbollah after the group struck Israeli troops with attack drones, and that it would continue regardless of Iranian threats. Lebanese President Aoun appealed to Israel to pursue talks rather than war.

sources.title: Lebanon's Aoun urges Israel to pursue talks, not war

stories.headline: China's demand restraint keeps oil below $100 amid Iran war and closed Strait of Hormuz, analysts warn buffer is fading
stories.summary: Brent settled around $94 after spiking to roughly $97.7 intraday on fresh Israel-Iran strikes, while WTI traded near $91-93. With the Strait of Hormuz closed since March, analysts told CNBC that softer Chinese buying and inventory draws have so far kept crude under triple digits, but warned the cushion is unlikely to hold if hostilities escalate or supply tightens further.

perspectives.keyFraming: A geopolitical risk premium is being capped, for now, by demand-side weakness in China.
perspectives.summary: Chinese demand softness and ample stocks are offsetting a war premium that would otherwise push Brent well above $100, analysts said, but they cautioned the buffer is thinning as the conflict reignites.

sources.title: China is helping to cushion global oil prices below $100 — but analysts warn it won't last

perspectives.keyFraming: The administration treats preserving a fragile ceasefire as the key backstop against an energy-price spiral.
perspectives.summary: Coverage tied the price path to the Trump administration's push to keep a fragile ceasefire alive, with the US blockade of Hormuz framed as staying in force until a final deal — the swing variable for energy inflation.

sources.title: Oil prices whipsaw on US-Iran-Israel strikes and OPEC

stories.headline: France and Germany abandon joint FCAS next-generation fighter jet program after Dassault-Airbus deadlock
stories.summary: France and Germany agreed Monday to abandon the joint Future Combat Air System (FCAS) fighter program, launched in 2017 to replace the Rafale and Eurofighter, after Merz and Macron failed to resolve disputes between Dassault and Airbus over workshare and requirements. France had pressed for a nuclear-capable, carrier-suitable jet. The two say they will keep cooperating on the networked 'Combat Cloud' layer, but the split is a significant blow to European defense-industrial integration.

perspectives.keyFraming: A flagship Franco-German defense project collapses over industrial control and requirements.
perspectives.summary: German officials said the two governments could not resolve the FCAS dispute, ending the joint fighter while preserving broader 'system-of-systems' cooperation. France's insistence on a nuclear- and carrier-capable jet and Dassault-Airbus workshare fights drove the split.

sources.title: Germany and France Drop Joint Fighter Jet Project, German Officials Say

perspectives.keyFraming: The breakup is a setback for European strategic autonomy but leaves a networked-defense path open.
perspectives.summary: European defense outlets reported the joint fighter is ending while Combat Cloud work continues, framing it as a blow to EU defense cooperation just as the continent faces pressure to rearm.

sources.title: Germany and France move to end joint FCAS fighter project while keeping wider European system-of-systems work

stories.headline: Nvidia and SK Hynix sign multiyear partnership through 2030 to co-develop next-generation AI-factory memory
stories.summary: Nvidia and SK Hynix announced a multiyear technology partnership running through 2030 to co-develop memory for AI factories, spanning Vera Rubin supercomputers, Vera CPUs, RTX-powered PCs and Jetson Thor robotics. Terms were undisclosed. The deal helped lift Nvidia shares roughly 2% Monday as chip stocks rebounded from Friday's Broadcom-driven rout, with the SOXX semiconductor ETF posting its best day in over a year.

perspectives.keyFraming: Nvidia is locking up the AI memory supply chain and deepening Korea's role in the hardware stack.
perspectives.summary: The pact secures HBM-class memory capacity for Nvidia's buildout and extends its co-development reach from data-center systems to PCs and robotics, analysts said.

sources.title: Nvidia, SK Hynix Sign Multi-Year Deal to Develop Next-Gen AI Memory Chips

perspectives.keyFraming: A blue-chip supply deal reassures markets that the AI capex thesis is intact after Friday's scare.
perspectives.summary: Coverage framed the partnership as evidence that hyperscale AI investment remains durable despite the Broadcom-driven sentiment shock that hammered chip stocks on Friday.

sources.title: NVIDIA and SK hynix Announce Multiyear Technology Partnership to Advance Memory for AI Factories
